steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence already has a main verb: "analyzed." The blank must introduce a verb form that modifies "researchers" without creating a second competing main verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "were looking": A finite verb form; creates two main verbs without a conjunction. ✗
  • "looked": Also finite; same structural error. ✗
  • "looking": A participle — non-finite, so it modifies "researchers" while leaving "analyzed" as the sole main verb. ✓
  • "had looked": Finite past perfect; same two-main-verb problem. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
